prevalence.trend: stable; improved diagnosis with genetic testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high per-patient disability (progressive ataxia, cardiomyopathy)

vulnerablePopulations: patients without access to neurogenetics services
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Progressive gait/limb ataxia with neurologic signs (areflexia, dysarthria) plus genetic confirmation of FXN expansion; evaluate cardiomyopathy (ECG/echo) and diabetes risk

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis after neurologic presentation and referral
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Limited (no cure); supportive care can improve function and reduce complications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Neurology referral and access to genetic testing; diagnostic delays common outside major centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Multidisciplinary care (physiotherapy, mobility aids, speech therapy), cardiology management, diabetes screening, psychosocial support; clinical trial referral when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited neurogenetics availability and rehab resources in some regions; transition care gaps
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Supportive multidisciplinary interventions can be implemented once diagnosed
